role_delineation_check: The Social Worker manages the clinical discharge destination and long-term placement logistics, while the Navigator verifies Arthur's demographic data in Atlantis, educates the family on transition support, and flags any changes in home-readiness sentiment to the clinical team.
narrative_summary: Arthur Bennett, a 75-year-old recovering from cardiac bypass surgery, initially planned to return home to his daughter's care following his rehabilitation stay. However, a series of post-operative dizzy spells led the clinical team to determine that his safety requirements exceeded what a home environment could provide. The Navigator acted as a supportive liaison during this shift, documenting the family's concerns in Atlantis and closing the case once the Social Worker confirmed a transition to a long-term care setting.
demographic_audit_note: I compared the facility face sheet to the Atlantis record and identified a discrepancy in the primary zip code; I updated the record in Atlantis to ensure the daughter's contact information was accurate for future coordination.
v_card_flyer_status: During an initial visit, I provided the program flyer to Arthur's daughter and assisted her in saving our contact information as a V-Card to ensure she would recognize our caller ID during the transition planning phase.
pre_dc_pulse_call: I attempted a pulse call to gauge the family's readiness, but the daughter expressed significant anxiety, stating the Social Worker was currently discussing a permanent facility placement instead of a home discharge.
atlantis_final_sync: I recorded the Social Worker's confirmation that Arthur would be transitioning to long-term care; the file was closed in Atlantis and no medical assistant visit was scheduled.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Social Worker informed me that Arthur was making steady progress and that the initial plan was for a home discharge with nursing support; I documented this in Atlantis and introduced the program to the family.

Arthur's daughter mentioned during a bedside visit that her father had suffered a fall during physical therapy; I noted her increased anxiety regarding home safety in Atlantis and alerted the Social Worker to her concerns.

The Social Worker called to provide a verbal update that the clinical team has pivoted the goal to long-term care due to Arthur's high fall risk; I documented the transition to a permanent facility and finalized the neutral closure in Atlantis.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter asks if her father will need a hospital bed at home after his bypass surgery.
format_2_triples.action_taken: I informed the daughter that the Social Worker and the clinical team coordinate all medical equipment needs and I documented her question in Atlantis for their awareness.

format_2_triples.situation: I noticed the Atlantis record lacked the daughter's secondary contact number which was listed on the facility face sheet.
format_2_triples.action_taken: I updated the demographic profile in Atlantis to include the secondary number to ensure the team could reach the family reliably.

format_2_triples.situation: The Social Worker has not responded to my inquiry regarding the home health agency's acceptance of the referral.
format_2_triples.action_taken: I documented the communication gap in the Atlantis timeline and waited for the Social Worker to provide a verbal status update.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Upon hearing from the daughter that the discharge plan is in flux, the Navigator waits for the Social Worker to provide a formal update and records the current uncertainty in the Atlantis timeline.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the Social Worker's ownership of the discharge plan and avoids providing conflicting information to the family before a decision is finalized.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator asks the Social Worker for a verbal status update on the transition plan and documents the shift from home care to long-term care in Atlantis to ensure the record reflects the current reality.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This allows the Navigator to maintain an accurate record and stay aligned with the facility's clinical goals without overstepping into placement logistics.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator calls local long-term care facilities to check on bed availability for Arthur to help the Social Worker speed up the placement process.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the no-vendor rule and interferes with the Social Worker's professional responsibilities regarding facility placement and clinical handoffs.
